free_text: Neutron and gamma rays from the alpha-particle
bombardment of 9Be, 10B, 11B, 13C and 18O
Reaction code changed in sub.012,013
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
The accuracy of data presentation has been revised in
all Subents.
Target was about 10 keV thick to 2 MeV alphas
The neutron detector used was
modified long-counter with paraffin moderator 5 inches
in diam. and 5 inches long surrounding a BF3 counter
which was 1 inch in diam. and extended to the front
surface of the paraffin.
A cross section of
0.40 barn at 5.3 MeV and 0.13 barn at 1.9 MeV are given
Statistical errors are less than or equal to the
diameter of the points.
No information about source of uncertainty
Fig. 1
SD: BIB updated. EN-ERR -> EN-ERR-DIG;
DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG; DATA-ERR1 -> DATA-ERR.
Target was about 10 keV thick to 2 MeV alphas
The neutron detector used was
modified long-counter with paraffin moderator 5 inches
in diam. and 5 inches long surrounding a BF3 counter
which was 1 inch in diam. and extended to the front
surface of the paraffin.
No information about source of uncertainty
Table I
SD: BIB updated.
Target was about 10 keV thick to 2 MeV alphas
A 1 in. x 1 in. NaI crystal mounted on a
DuMont 6292 photomultiplier tube was used for the
gamma-ray detector.
Gamma-ray cross sections were obtained by using the
yield of 4.4-MeV gamma-rays from standardized RaD-Be
source assuming 0.6 gamma-rays per neutrons.
Statistical errors are less than or equal to the
diameter of the points.
No information about source of uncertainty
Fig. 1
SD: SF5=PAR added to REACTION code. BIB
updated. EN-ERR -> EN-ERR-DIG; DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG;
DATA-ERR1 -> DATA-ERR. Col.E added to COMMON section.
Target was about 10 keV thick to 2 MeV alphas
A 1 in. x 1 in. NaI crystal mounted on a
DuMont 6292 photomultiplier tube was used for the
gamma-ray detector.
Gamma-ray cross sections were obtained by using the
yield of 4.4-MeV gamma-rays from standardized RaD-Be
source assuming 0.6 gamma-rays per neutrons.
No information about source of uncertainty
Table I
SD: SF5=PAR added to REACTION code. BIB
updated. E=4.4 MeV added.
Table I
SD: STATUS updated.
67% enriched 13C target was 20 keV
thick for 3.0 MeV alphas
The neutron detector used was
modified long-counter with paraffin moderator 5 inches
in diam. and 5 inches long surrounding a BF3 counter
which was 1 inch in diam. and extended to the front
surface of the paraffin.
A 1 in. x 1 in. NaI crystal mounted on a
DuMont 6292 photomultiplier tube was used for the
gamma-ray detector.
It is estimated that at most 10% of the gamma-ray
yield could be caused by neutrons in the NaI crystal.
The cross section calibration was made at 1.9-MeV
resonance using both weighed evaporated Be targets
on 0.002 in. aluminum foil and a thick Be target. A
stopping power of 1.40 MeV/mg/cm2 was used for 1.9 MeV
alphas in 9Be.
Statistical errors are less than or equal to the
diameter of the points on the fig.
Fig. 2
SD:  BIB updated. EN-ERR -> EN-ERR-DIG;
DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG.
67% enriched 13C target was 20 keV
thick for 3.0 MeV alphas
The neutron detector used was

which was 1 inch in diam. and extended to the front
surface of the paraffin.
A 1 in. x 1 in. NaI crystal mounted on a
DuMont 6292 photomultiplier tube was used for the
gamma-ray detector.
It is estimated that at most 10% of the gamma-ray
yield could be caused by neutrons in the NaI crystal.
The cross section calibration was made at 1.9-MeV
resonance using both weighed evaporated Be targets
on 0.002 in. aluminum foil and a thick Be target. A
stopping power of 1.40 MeV/mg/cm2 was used for 1.9 MeV
alphas in 9Be.

96% enriched 10B target was 10 keV
thick for 2.0 MeV alphas
A 1 in. x 1 in. NaI crystal mounted on a
DuMont 6292 photomultiplier tube was used for the
gamma-ray detector.
The NaI counter was biased in such a manner that
it would efficiently detect the 3.68 and 3.86 MeV
gamma ray from 12C
Statistical errors are less than or equal to the
diameter of the points.

An evaporated normal boron target was 15 keV thick for
2.0 MeV alphas
The neutron detector used was

in diam. and 5 inches long surrounding a BF3 counter
which was 1 inch in diam. and extended to the front
surface of the paraffin.
A 1 in. x 1 in. NaI crystal mounted on a

gamma-ray detector.
The NaI counter was biased in such a manner that it
would efficiently detect the 3.68 and 3.86 MeV gamma

diameter of the points.

An evaporated normal boron target was 15 keV thick for
2.0 MeV alphas
The neutron detector used was

Table IV
SD: BIB updated.
Table IV
SD: STATUS updated.
The targets were prepared by heating a tungsten disk
in induction heater with a sample of enriched 18O (40%)
The target thickness varies from 90 keV to 130keV.
The neutron detector used was
modified long-counter with paraffin moderator 5 inches
in diam. and 5 inches long surrounding a BF3 counter
which was 1 inch in diam. and extended to the front
surface of the paraffin.
A 1 in. x 1 in. NaI crystal mounted on a
DuMont 6292 photomultiplier tube was used for the
gamma-ray detector.
The cross section was obtained by estimating the
amount of oxygen in the target from the yield
obtained in measuring the 16O(d,n) threshold.
Statistical errors are less than or equal to the
diameter of the points.
No information about source of uncertainty
Fig. 6
SD: BIB updated. EN-ERR -> EN-ERR-DIG;
DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG; DATA-ERR1 -> DATA-ERR.
The targets were prepared by heating a tungsten disk
in induction heater with a sample of enriched 18O (40%)
The target thickness varies from 90 keV to 130keV.
The neutron detector used was
modified long-counter with paraffin moderator 5 inches
in diam. and 5 inches long surrounding a BF3 counter
which was 1 inch in diam. and extended to the front
surface of the paraffin.
A 1 in. x 1 in. NaI crystal mounted on a
DuMont 6292 photomultiplier tube was used for the
gamma-ray detector.
The cross section was obtained by estimating the
amount of oxygen in the target from the yield
obtained in measuring the 16O(d,n) threshold.
